mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound persistently compresses the equity discount rate, sustaining elevated valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as capital seeks risk assets in a low-yield environment.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from an elevated post-shock level (UNRATE > 6%), it signals an accelerating labor market recovery that supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months while UNRATE level > 6%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.7 elevated relative to historical norms
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases, or rank IC conditional on trigger is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicate broad-based consumer and residential investment demand recovery, a combination that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter more reliably than either series alone because it confirms demand strength is not sector-isolated.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM > 0 and HOUST MoM > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal's 60-day forward hit rate is not significantly higher than the hit rate using RSAFS or HOUST alone (difference in proportions test p > 0.10), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which propagates quickly into short-term equity sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant correlation with the sign of INDPRO MoM change (rank IC < 0.02) over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime combining near-zero policy rates (FEDFUNDS below 1%) with low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL MoM change near zero), the absence of near-term tightening risk removes a key downside catalyst for equities, producing positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon as investors price in extended accommodation.
regime_hint: zero rate lower bound with low inflation regime
failure_condition: Conditional on the regime trigger being active, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the regime-conditional rank IC does not exceed the unconditional rank IC by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A turn from negative to positive month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) after a period of decline signals a labor market trough and marks the beginning of an economic recovery phase, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as markets re-rate growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change turns positive after at least 2 consecutive prior months of decline
failure_condition: In triggered instances the 20-day forward return hit rate for positive SPX outcomes falls below 55%, or rank IC conditional on trigger is not distinguishable from zero at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly confirm an active recessionary demand shock, which depresses forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either weak GDP or rising unemployment predicts in isolation.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal fails to produce a 60-day forward negative-return hit rate significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10), or its predictive power is not superior to using GDPC1 or UNRATE independently.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that raises recession risk, triggering rapid de-risking in equity markets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 5-day forward SPX returns are negative in fewer than 55% of cases, or the conditional rank IC is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10) across rolling 2-year windows.
